subgoals: Endpoint shadow decomposition: for x in {a_{n-1}, b_{n-1}}, decompose P_O(u,x) into primitive arcs/segments e and assign pairwise disjoint shadow intervals I_e subset [u,x] whose total length is at most |u-x|.

Terminal charge allocation: split [u,x] cap O_n into nonnegative charges c_e assigned to nearby primitive pieces, with sum_e c_e = L_n(u,x), allowing separate 0/1/2-crossing cases of the chord with O_n.
Local charged-shadow inequality: for every allowed primitive piece e in an endpoint chain with radius ratios bounded by rho_upper, prove |e| + c_e <= (643/250)|I_e| minus a nonnegative local loss.

Endpoint strict-loss lemma: for x=a_{n-1} and x=b_{n-1}, at least one terminal or initial primitive piece has loss bounded below by a positive B_target-scale quantity, so equality in the summed inequality is impossible.
Summation lemma: summing the local inequalities yields |P_O(u,x)| + L_n(u,x) < (643/250)|u-x| for both endpoints x, which is exactly S2.
obstruction: The hard point is proving an ordered disjoint shadow decomposition; boundary paths may backtrack relative to [u,x], and terminal charges from L_n may overburden pieces with very small chord shadow.

subgoals: Define the lookahead endpoint reserve for a prefix C ending at O_m and an admissible next disk T by R_C^T(p) = (643/250)|u-p| - |P_{C union T}(u,p)| - L_T(u,p), for p in {a_m,b_m}.

Two-disk base lemma: for m=1 and every admissible lookahead disk T with radius ratio at most rho_upper, both reserves R_C^T(a_1) and R_C^T(b_1) are strictly positive, with the chosen B_target margin except in degenerate zero-scale limits.

Right-end transfer lemma: assuming positive B_target-level reserves for {a_m,b_m} with lookahead T, prove the reserve for a_T remains positive after appending T and choosing an arbitrary admissible successor S.
Left-end transfer lemma: prove the analogous transfer to b_T, using the exact connector a_T b_T comparison and a Lipschitz bound for the change of the segment term L_S.
Local transfer verification: reduce the two transfer lemmas to finite two- or three-variable inequalities in adjacent overlap angles/radius ratios, retaining the L_S term explicitly throughout.
Induction conclusion: apply the strengthened invariant to C=O_{1,n-1} and T=O_n to obtain R_C^T(a_{n-1})>0 and R_C^T(b_{n-1})>0, equivalent to S2.
obstruction: The reserve invariant must be chosen carefully: a uniform margin may fail in near-degenerate configurations, and the transfer inequalities may require genuinely multidimensional optimization rather than simple one-variable certificates.
